codeReview.summary: Reviewed the fix feature metadata in `/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/features.json`, the fix handoff/transcript for session `4027b4e6-575a-4e32-a2cb-d2d23ce2b903`, the prior failed review at `/home/lenstr/workspace/personal/hack/rgo-lang/.factory/validation/misc-callback-followups/scrutiny/reviews/misc-typed-nonvoid-lambda-return-lowering.json`, the original feature handoff/transcript for session `b5e4a5df-6bf3-49b5-9036-ff42f9830682`, the `compiler-worker` skill, and both commits `c7ecc0b5d40983203adab1341ffd5e3b9d6be9dd` and `81ebb9244a4e5918c1d30a691ecd7e1e5447351b`. The fix closes the two regressions called out in the previous review (empty-body and void statement-only typed non-void lambdas), but it still does not fully enforce the feature requirement to reject typed non-void lambda bodies that fail to guarantee a value. A trailing block-like statement is now treated as guaranteed purely by syntax kind, so unsupported non-value forms can still typecheck and lower into non-void Go func literals without a guaranteed return.

issues.description: The new `has_guaranteed_value` heuristic still accepts some typed non-void lambdas whose bodies do not guarantee a value. In the `body.final_expr = None` path, any trailing `StmtExpr (ExprIf _ | ExprMatch _ | ExprBlock _)` is treated as value-producing unconditionally (`lib/typecheck.ml:574-582`), even though `check_if` still returns `TVoid` for `if` without `else` (`lib/typecheck.ml:1450-1459`) and a trailing block expression can also remain `TVoid`. Codegen then still routes the last `StmtExpr` through `gen_final_expr_as_return` (`lib/codegen.ml:4211-4216`), and `ExprIf` without `else` is emitted without a fallback return (`lib/codegen.ml:2266-2280`). That recreates the original typecheck/codegen misalignment for bodies such as `let f = |x: i32| -> i32 { if x > 0 { x } };` or `let f = |x: i32| -> i32 { { println("x"); } };`, which still fail to guarantee a value but are not rejected by this fix.

The added negative regressions only cover the empty-body and `println` statement-only cases (`test/test_typecheck.ml:3197-3204`, `test/test_codegen.ml:3675-3679`). There is still no regression coverage for the remaining unsupported no-value shapes above, such as a trailing `if` without `else` or a trailing void block expression in a typed non-void lambda, so the surviving mismatch is not locked down by tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `compiler-worker` skill still says to write tests first and to record deviations honestly, but the fix worker edited `lib/typecheck.ml` multiple times before later adding the new negative tests while the handoff still reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

Transcript skeleton line 75 in `/home/lenstr/.factory/missions/0fca519c-8629-4615-81ce-b9d60fb3b0e7/worker-transcripts.jsonl` for session `4027b4e6-575a-4e32-a2cb-d2d23ce2b903` shows several `Edit` calls on `lib/typecheck.ml` before the later `Edit` calls that add cases in `test/test_typecheck.ml` and `test/test_codegen.ml`, while the handoff JSON records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Fail. The fix correctly rejects empty-body and void statement-only typed non-void lambdas, but it still accepts other non-value trailing block forms (notably `if` without `else` and void block expressions), so the original blocking typecheck/codegen mismatch is only partially addressed.
